title: Trump Finally Goes to the White House Press Dinner—But No Comedian!
summary:
For years, President Trump refused to go to the White House Correspondents' Dinner—a big fancy party where the president and journalists eat together. But this year, he finally showed up! It was his first time attending as president, after skipping it five times before.

Why did he skip it before? Trump has often called the media "the enemy of the people" and sued news organizations. So going to a dinner with journalists was a big deal.

This year's dinner was different. Usually, a comedian makes jokes—sometimes making fun of the president. But this time, there was no comedian. Some people think that's because Trump didn't want to be roasted.

Journalist groups wrote an open letter asking the dinner's host to send a "forthright message" to Trump about protecting press freedom. They said freedom of the press is not a partisan issue (meaning it shouldn't be about Democrats vs. Republicans).

Trump has been to this dinner once before—in 2011, before he ran for president. Back then, President Obama made fun of him for pushing the "birther" lie (the false claim that Obama wasn't born in the U.S.). Comedian Seth Meyers also cracked jokes. Trump sat there with a straight face. People say that night motivated him to run for president in 2016.

So this year, Trump finally got his chance to be at the dinner as the most powerful person in the room. What did he say? We'll have to wait and see!
why_it_matters: This story shows that the way the president and journalists treat each other affects what news we get and how free reporters are to tell the truth.

keywords.explanation: Journalists who report news for newspapers, TV, or websites.
A person who tells jokes and makes people laugh for a living.
Something that is connected to a political party rather than being neutral.
Taken to court to try to get a judge to decide who is right.
A person who falsely claimed that President Obama was not born in the United States.
Gave someone a strong reason to do something.

questions.question: Why did President Trump skip the White House Correspondents' Dinner for five times?
options: He called the media 'the enemy of the people'
questions.correct_answer: He called the media 'the enemy of the people'

questions.question: What was different about this year's dinner compared to previous years?
options: There was no comedian making jokes
questions.correct_answer: There was no comedian making jokes

questions.question: What did journalist groups ask the dinner's host to do in an open letter?
options: Send a 'forthright message' to Trump about protecting press freedom
questions.correct_answer: Send a 'forthright message' to Trump about protecting press freedom

questions.question: What false claim did Trump push about President Obama in 2011?
options: That Obama was not a U.S. citizen
That Obama was born in Canada
That Obama was a secret agent
questions.correct_answer: That Obama was not a U.S. citizen

questions.question: Who made fun of Trump at the 2011 dinner?
options: Both President Obama and Seth Meyers
questions.correct_answer: Both President Obama and Seth Meyers

questions.question: What do some people think motivated Trump to run for president in 2016?
options: The jokes made about him at the 2011 dinner
questions.correct_answer: The jokes made about him at the 2011 dinner

background_read: The White House Correspondents' Dinner (a fancy party where the president eats dinner with journalists and celebrities) has been held every year since 1920.
Usually a comedian tells jokes, and sometimes they make fun of the president — that's part of the tradition.
President Trump has often said bad things about the media, so it was a surprise when he decided to attend this year.
Article_Structure: WHO: President Donald Trump, journalists, and comedians (especially Seth Meyers).
WHAT: Trump attended the White House Correspondents' Dinner for the first time as president, but there was no comedian to roast him.
WHERE: The dinner is held in Washington D.C. at the White House or a nearby hotel.
WHY: Trump had skipped the dinner five times because he calls the media 'the enemy of the people,' but this year he finally went.

perspectives.description: They are happy he attended and see it as a sign he is willing to work with the media on his own terms.
They are cautious and want Trump to respect press freedom; the open letter shows they are worried about attacks on news organizations.
They notice that Trump avoided being roasted by having no comedian, and they remember the 2011 dinner as the event that may have launched his political career.
